description: Canonical structure for Decision Brief. The validator at scripts/validate-brief.py enforces this spec against DECISION-BRIEF.md. This is the single source of truth; references/output-format.md must match it. v1.1 additions: positions 15 (Appendix C: Complete Effects Map) and 16 (Sources), content_checks block (unsourced numbers + depends-on mirror), trailing-block ref format, legacy inline ref fallback. v1.2: Appendix B and C swapped — Complete Effects Map is now Appendix B (required in full/medium), Quick Mode vs Full Loop Comparison is now Appendix C (optional, only emitted when a quick run exists for the same slug). Keeps appendix letters alphabetical when the optional section is absent.

required_content.desc: Recommendation: line (recommendation is called out here but full block is at section 12)

sections.description: Must cover 5+ of: strongest analysis, weakest analysis, key disagreement, uncertainty hotspot, consensus surprises, blind spots caught
Only include if a single-persona insight became the recommendation

cross_references.name: worst_cases_preserved
cross_references.description: At least 60% of worst_cases scenario_ids in source JSON must appear as ref comments in the brief's Worst Cases subsection

cross_references.name: black_swans_preserved
cross_references.description: The Black Swans subsection must contain at least as many paragraphs/bullets as the source JSON has entries. Use count if IDs absent.

cross_references.name: irrational_actors_preserved
cross_references.description: At least one irrational actor scenario must be present. Count-based because legacy runs lack IDs.

cross_references.name: convergence_iterations_present
cross_references.description: Table must have one row per iteration in convergence-log.json

sections.description: All effects beyond the top 15 by council_agreement × probability shown in section 4 (Effects Map) go here. Keeps the main body scannable while preserving the full possibility map as a machine-readable artifact. One table covering every effect_id not already in section 4.

sections.header: ## Appendix C: Quick Mode vs Full Loop Comparison
sections.description: Only emitted when a quick mode run exists for the same decision slug. Summarizes what the full loop surfaced that the quick run missed (additional hypotheses, joint probabilities, irrational actor analysis). Independent COMPARISON-VS-QUICK.md file is a separate artifact, not part of the brief.

Every dollar figure, percentage, elasticity estimate, market size, or other specific numeric claim made anywhere in the brief must trace to either: (a) a ground-data.md citation [G#], (b) a user-inputs.md claim [U#], or (c) a council estimate with persona tag [C#:persona]. Missing sourcing = HARD_FAIL. Claims without any of those three must be rewritten as qualitative ('acquisition likely increases meaningfully') instead of quoting a fake number.

source_tags.G: Ground data (from Phase 1 WebSearch) — link to URL from ground-data.md
source_tags.C: Council estimate (from Phase 3 personas) — must name the persona and iteration

prohibited_patterns.name: snake_case_in_prose
prohibited_patterns.description: Snake_case identifier appearing outside code blocks, inline code, tables, or links. Use the description field from source JSON, not the key.

prohibited_patterns.name: raw_effect_id_in_prose
prohibited_patterns.description: Backticked raw effect_id or assumption_key. Always use the human description.

primary_format.name: trailing_block
primary_format.example:
<!-- validator-refs:
worst_cases: wc1_a, wc2_b
black_swans: bs1_c
irrational_actors: ia1_d
effects: acq_increase, churn_reduction
-->
primary_format.location: Single occurrence at the end of DECISION-BRIEF.md. One category per line. IDs comma-separated, exactly as they appear in source JSON.

legacy_format.name: inline_comment
legacy_format.description: Legacy inline format. Validator still parses it for backward compatibility. New briefs MUST use the trailing block.

reference_comment_pattern.description: The validator confirms which items from adversary.json, effects-chains.json, etc. made it into the brief by reading either the trailing <!-- validator-refs: ... --> block (new briefs) or inline <!-- ref:ID --> comments (legacy briefs). Both are HTML comments, invisible in rendered markdown.

unsourced_dollar_figure.description: Any dollar figure ($10M, $800M, $50/seat) or specific percentage (25%, 88-94%) or multiplier (2x, 3:1) in the brief MUST have an adjacent inline source tag [G#], [D#], [U#], or [C#:persona]. Figures without a tag are fabricated — the validator flags them HARD_FAIL.
unsourced_dollar_figure.note: Exempt sections either list raw grounding data, the sources table itself, mechanical scores, or timeline markers like 'Month 3'. Everywhere else, numbers need a tag within 120 chars.

depends_on_missing_assumption.description: Every bullet in the Recommendation section's 'Depends on:' line must correspond to an assumption row in Key Assumptions (position 10). If the Recommendation names an assumption that doesn't appear in the table, either the table is missing a load-bearing assumption (add it) or the Recommendation is citing a phantom (rewrite).
depends_on_missing_assumption.threshold: every depends-on item must match at least one Key Assumptions description via substring or semantic token overlap > 60%

synthesis_dedup_skipped.description: Synthesis must merge effects across personas via mechanical merge (exact effect_id) AND semantic dedup of novel IDs (see references/persona-council.md 'Synthesis Pass' and 'Mechanical merge' / 'Semantic dedup' steps). When dedup is skipped, each persona's near-duplicate effects sit as separate rows with council_agreement=1 each, instead of merging into one row with council_agreement>=2. The brief then shows inflated 1st-order effect counts and meaningless agreement signals — the visualization fans out to 25-30 dots per hypothesis, the Effects Map's High-Confidence section under-fills, and the Judge's effects_delta metric loses its meaning. This check reads iteration-{latest}/effects-chains.json and computes the average council_agreement across all 1st-order effects. Below the minimum threshold, synthesis was skipped or under-applied and the run must be re-synthesized before the brief is published.
synthesis_dedup_skipped.remediation: Re-run the synthesis step from references/persona-council.md 'Synthesis Pass'. Specifically perform Step 3 (Semantic dedup) — either inline if <=3 novel IDs OR by spawning a single dedup agent if >3. Then regenerate effects-chains.json and re-run the validator.
